Magli guided Rebecca through her strengths and then gave her some directives to do at home. She was asked to keep a journal of her skin picking, including when and how long the picking took place. So this is four phases of shifting a bad habit. Now in the first phase, we regulate the bad habit, the skin picking. When something the client is doing, they perceive to be out of their control. Like I can't stop myself. I can't help myself. It just happens to me. The first thing you can do is regulate it. Change it in just one way. This is something that Milton Erickson taught us is that if you change any behavior, you can change any behavior by changing one thing to it. You add it or subtract it. What we're doing in effect is interrupting the pattern. For example, Maggley gave the directive to keep a journal of her skin picking. She's not saying stop the skin picking. She's saying keep a journal. Adding that to her skin picking regulates the habit of skin picking. She also asked Rebecca to pick the skin of her doll instead of her own skin. So she's not saying stop. She's saying pick the skin of her doll. When she feels like picking her own skin, she could pick the doll's skin instead. So that's phase one. That's regulate the bad habit. Now that we've regulated the habit and changed it, phase two is to replace the habit with new rituals, new more sustainable habits that Rebecca can carry out on an ongoing basis. And this is like replacing skin picking with better things that she can do to care for her skin. So Maggley will be giving Rebecca new ways to spend time caring for a skin that does not have to do with picking. The third phase is to stabilize relationships. When Rebecca follows a directive to write down every time she picks on her skin and for when and for how long and then on her own initiative she brings a stopwatch with her to the washroom, this completely replaces her former unconscious spontaneous habit. Phase two, replace the bad habit. Because people are used to using their bad habits to meet their needs, they need other ways to meet those needs.
